evals.prompt: hey this mlflow experiment at http://127.0.0.1:5001/#/experiments/292891157014984425 is failing like crazy, can you figure out what's going wrong? the artifacts are on cyy2 server
evals.expected_output: Comprehensive failure analysis report with error categorization, configuration patterns, and actionable recommendations

assertions.name: uses_mlflow_reader_skill
assertions.description: Explicitly uses the mlflow-reader skill for run discovery

assertions.name: fetches_remote_artifacts
assertions.description: Uses SCP to retrieve error logs and configuration files from cyy2 server

assertions.name: categorizes_errors
assertions.description: Identifies specific error types like CUDA/NVML errors and k-fold validation issues

assertions.name: analyzes_configurations
assertions.description: Detects patterns in failed configurations such as k-fold setup problems

assertions.name: structured_report
assertions.description: Generates report following the required markdown template with all sections

assertions.name: actionable_recommendations
assertions.description: Provides specific, implementable fixes based on identified error patterns

evals.prompt: our team's been running experiments for the past week and we've got about 200 failed runs in experiment 'model-sweep-2024'. the mlflow server is at ml-server.company.com:5000 and artifacts are stored on compute-cluster.research.edu in /shared/experiments/mlruns/. need to understand the failure patterns before our next sprint planning meeting tomorrow
evals.expected_output: Detailed analysis report focusing on failure patterns and trends suitable for sprint planning

assertions.name: handles_custom_server_config
assertions.description: Adapts to custom MLflow server and artifact storage configuration

assertions.name: processes_large_failure_set
assertions.description: Efficiently handles analysis of 200+ failed runs

assertions.name: identifies_sprint_priorities
assertions.description: Categorizes issues by priority for sprint planning context

assertions.name: provides_trend_analysis
assertions.description: Identifies patterns across the week-long experiment period

assertions.name: business_context_aware
assertions.description: Tailors recommendations for sprint planning meeting context

evals.prompt: i'm looking at this sweep-all-20260309-040454 experiment with tons of failures. server is running on port 5001 locally, but the actual run data is stored on our compute cluster at cluster.research.edu. need to know if it's a config issue or infrastructure problem - we're seeing some weird CUDA errors but also validation metric issues
evals.expected_output: Analysis distinguishing between configuration vs infrastructure issues with specific focus on CUDA and validation problems

assertions.name: distinguishes_error_categories
assertions.description: Clearly separates infrastructure issues (CUDA) from configuration issues (validation)

assertions.name: provides_root_cause_analysis
assertions.description: Identifies whether problems are systemic infrastructure vs configuration-related

assertions.name: handles_mixed_error_types
assertions.description: Analyzes both CUDA/hardware errors and validation metric configuration issues

assertions.name: cluster_environment_aware
assertions.description: Considers multi-server cluster environment in analysis

assertions.name: prioritizes_by_impact
assertions.description: Ranks issues by whether they're blocking all experiments vs subset
